one_liner: Binance offers a broad, self-serve REST/WebSocket API for spot trading and market data secured via Ed25519/RSA/HMAC signed requests, with several community-built MCP servers already available.

auth_methods.notes: Ed25519 (recommended), RSA, or HMAC (deprecated) key pairs used to sign requests; API key generated via Binance account API Management section.
WebSocket session.logon authentication method to avoid re-sending signature per request.

self_serve_notes: Free and self-serve for verified accounts: create account, complete KYC, enable 2FA, then generate API keys via API Management. No subscription or per-call fees; only standard trading fees apply on executed trades.

api_surface.notes: Extensive REST and WebSocket API covering spot trading, market data, account management, and hundreds of endpoints across Binance products.

main_blocker: Requires KYC-verified Binance account and generated API keys (Ed25519/RSA/HMAC) before programmatic access; HMAC deprecated so agents should support asymmetric key signing.

evidence.supports: Describes Ed25519 (recommended), RSA, and deprecated HMAC API key types and signing approach.
REST API documentation showing broad endpoint coverage and request security/signing details.
Confirms free, self-serve API access with no subscription fees, only standard trading fees.
Describes steps to create API keys via API Management after KYC and 2FA.

flags: KYC/identity verification required before API key generation
HMAC auth deprecated in favor of Ed25519/RSA
Multiple unofficial third-party MCP server implementations exist, no official Binance MCP server
